content: We can reframe the general design of goal-seeking agents around a modular intrinsic curiosity 'core' that dynamically reshapes an agent’s active memory based on prediction error, rather than just using curiosity as an auxiliary reward signal for narrow exploration tasks. This means any agent (not just reinforcement learning agents) can be made continuously adaptive by having its ICM module prune under-explored memory pathways and consolidate high-prediction-error knowledge as new long-term agent memory.
